task_completion.description: The agent opened or read the measurement_targets.txt file to obtain the pixel coordinates and aperture radius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal output (e.g., cat command), or script reading the contents of ~/AstroImages/optical_depth/measurement_targets.txt.

task_completion.description: The agent opened the 656nmos.fits image file to prepare for measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image analysis tool (like AstroImageJ, DS9) displaying the FITS image, or a Python script/terminal loading the FITS file.

task_completion.description: The agent measured the mean pixel values for both the Pillar Core and Background Emission using the specified coordinates and aperture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement tool dialogs, region of interest (ROI) circles drawn on the image, measurement result tables, or script output showing the extracted mean pixel values.

task_completion.description: The agent calculated the Transmission (T = I_pillar / I_background) and Optical Depth (tau = -ln(T)).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculator application, Python REPL, or script executing the division and natural logarithm (-ln) operations with the measured values.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the final results to optical_depth_report.txt in the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of the report file containing the four required lines: I_pillar, I_background, Transmission, and Optical_Depth with their respective values.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the FITS data to extract measurements rather than hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible use of measurement tools in an astronomy image viewer or execution of a script that explicitly reads the FITS data array at the given coordinates.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the math calculations rather than guessing the final values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a calculator, spreadsheet, or code execution showing the actual math operations (division and natural log) being computed.

integrity.description: The agent saved the report to the exact required directory path.
integrity.visual_evidence: A file manager, save dialog, or terminal command explicitly showing the path /home/ga/AstroImages/optical_depth/output/optical_depth_report.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be located exactly at /home/ga/AstroImages/optical_depth/output/optical_depth_report.txt. The data source is the HST Eagle Nebula H-alpha (656nm) FITS file from the ESA FITS Liberator.
